name: Benjamin Franklin
abstract: Benjamin Franklin (January 17, 1706 [O.S. January 6, 1705] – April 17, 1790) was an American polymath who was one of the Founding Fathers of the United States; a drafter and signer of the Declaration of Independence; and the first postmaster general.

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: They’re all in a dialogue, and Jesus keeps repeating the same thing: “The only sin is stupidity.” That’s some sin. ... Voltaire, Benjamin Franklin, and Jesus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: That was a printed postcard with Benjamin Franklin imprinted, a one-cent stamp in the corner.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: There was a ringer there, Benjamin Franklin. He left Boston before he graduated.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
